title: Getting Started with fireR
text: fireR provides convenient access USGS fire datasets EPA/CEC ecoregion boundaries: get_mtbs() / read_mtbs() MTBS (Monitoring Trends Burn Severity) wildfire perimeter data get_sefire() SE FireMap Annual Burn Severity Mosaics get_nal1eco(), get_nal2eco(), get_nal3eco() CEC North America ecoregion boundaries get_usl3eco(), get_usl4eco() US EPA ecoregion boundaries
code: library(fireR)

title: Getting Started with fireR
text: output = "sf", read_mtbs() returns sf object containing every fire perimeter MTBS composite dataset — years, states. Plot burn area acreage get quick sense data:
code: fires <- read_mtbs(output = "sf") fires #> Simple feature collection with 31,386 features and 22 fields #> Geometry type: MULTIPOLYGON #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: -178.3 ymin: 17.9 xmax: -65.3 ymax: 71.4 #> CRS:           NAD83 / Conus Albers (EPSG:5070) plot(fires["BurnBndAc"], main = "MTBS Fire Perimeters — All Years")

title: Getting Started with fireR
text: Pass single integer keep fires started calendar year:
code: fires_2020 <- read_mtbs(years = 2020, output = "sf") nrow(fires_2020) #> [1] 246

title: Getting Started with fireR
text: Use R’s : operator keep fires within contiguous span years: Quickly visualise major fires last decade fell:
code: fires_recent <- read_mtbs(years = 2018:2023, output = "sf") nrow(fires_recent) #> [1] 1328 library(sf)  plot(st_geometry(fires_recent), col = "#E25822AA", border = NA,      main = "MTBS Fire Perimeters 2018–2023")

title: Getting Started with fireR
text: Supply vector individual years return fires exact years (non-contiguous):
code: fires_sel <- read_mtbs(years = c(2000, 2010, 2020), output = "sf")

title: Getting Started with fireR
text: Set output = "vect" ("terra") receive terra::SpatVector instead sf object — handy rest workflow uses terra:
code: library(terra)  fires_vect <- read_mtbs(years = 2020:2023, output = "vect") class(fires_vect) #> [1] "SpatVector" #> attr(,"package") #> [1] "terra"  plot(fires_vect, "BurnBndAc", main = "Recent Fire Perimeters (terra)")

title: Getting Started with fireR
text: Set geometry = FALSE drop geometry get plain data.frame. Useful need metadata (fire name, year, acreage, etc.):
code: tbl <- read_mtbs(geometry = FALSE) head(tbl[, c("Incid_Name", "Ig_Date", "BurnBndAc", "Incid_Type")]) #>     Incid_Name   Ig_Date BurnBndAc Incid_Type #> 1  LOWDEN FIRE 1984-07-01   4537.35   Wildfire #> 2  EUREKA FIRE 1984-08-15  11202.06   Wildfire #> ...

title: Getting Started with fireR
text: MTBS ZIP archive ~100 MB. Download get_mtbs(), read disk read_mtbs() every subsequent call.

title: Getting Started with fireR
text: Pass cache = TRUE read_mtbs() look ZIP tools::R_user_dir("fireR", "cache") — platform-appropriate user directory persists R sessions:
code: # Download once to the user cache directory get_mtbs(directory = tools::R_user_dir("fireR", "cache"))  # Read from cache on every subsequent call fires <- read_mtbs(cache = TRUE)

title: Getting Started with fireR
text: Supply directory path functions control file stored:
code: get_mtbs(directory = "~/data/mtbs_cache") fires <- read_mtbs(cache = "~/data/mtbs_cache")

title: Getting Started with fireR
text: USGS releases updated dataset, use overwrite = TRUE get_mtbs() bypass cache re-download:
code: get_mtbs(directory = tools::R_user_dir("fireR", "cache"), overwrite = TRUE) fires <- read_mtbs(cache = TRUE)

title: Getting Started with fireR
text: Progress messages printed default. Suppress verbose = FALSE:
code: fires <- read_mtbs(years = 2022, verbose = FALSE)

title: Getting Started with fireR
text: get_sefire() downloads Annual Burn Severity Mosaic ZIP archives southeastern United States (2000–2022). Pass single year, range, vector specific years:
code: # Single year zip_path <- get_sefire(2020) # Contiguous range zip_paths <- get_sefire(2015:2020, directory = "data/sefire") # Specific years only zip_paths <- get_sefire(c(2000, 2010, 2020))

title: Getting Started with fireR
text: Commission Environmental Cooperation (CEC) ecoregion framework divides North America hierarchical ecological units.
code: # Level 1 — broadest continental divisions na_l1 <- get_nal1eco()  # Level 2 — finer continental subdivisions na_l2 <- get_nal2eco()  # Level 3 — finest continental scale (= US EPA Level III) na_l3 <- get_nal3eco()  plot(na_l1["NA_L1NAME"], main = "North America Level 1 Ecoregions")

title: Getting Started with fireR
text: US EPA ecoregions available Levels 3 4, option include state boundaries polygons. ecoregion functions accept output = "vect" terra::SpatVector cache argument persist downloads across sessions:
code: # Level 3 — without state boundaries (default) us_l3 <- get_usl3eco()  # Level 3 — with state boundaries dissolved in us_l3_states <- get_usl3eco(state = TRUE)  # Level 4 — finest US subdivisions us_l4 <- get_usl4eco() us_l4_states <- get_usl4eco(state = TRUE) us_l3 <- get_usl3eco(output = "vect", cache = TRUE)

title: Getting Started with fireR
text: sf object, full sf dplyr ecosystem available .

title: Getting Started with fireR
code: library(dplyr)  fires_2000s <- read_mtbs(years = 2000:2023, output = "sf")  top10 <- fires_2000s |>   slice_max(BurnBndAc, n = 10) |>   select(Incid_Name, Ig_Date, BurnBndAc) |>   st_drop_geometry()  top10

what: Example: area burned and fires per year
title: Getting Started with fireR
code: library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(sf)  fires_all <- read_mtbs(output = "sf") fires_all |>   st_drop_geometry() |>   mutate(year = as.integer(substr(Ig_Date, 1, 4))) |>   group_by(year) |>   summarize(area_burned = sum(BurnBndAc)) |>   ggplot(aes(year, area_burned)) +   geom_col(fill = "#8B1A1A") +   labs(title = "Area Burned by Year",        x = "Year",        y = "Area Burned (Acres)") +   theme_classic() fires_all |>   st_drop_geometry() |>   mutate(year = as.integer(substr(Ig_Date, 1, 4))) |>   count(year) |>   ggplot(aes(year, n)) +   geom_col(fill = "#8B1A1A") +   labs(title = "Number of Fires by Year",        x = "Year",        y = "Number of Fires") +   theme_classic()

title: Getting Started with fireR
text: Monitoring Trends Burn Severity programme joint USGS / USFS initiative maps location, extent, burn severity large wildfires (>1 000 acres western US; >500 acres eastern US) across conterminous USA, Alaska, Hawaii, Puerto Rico 1984 present. information: https://www.mtbs.gov/

what: fireR • USGS Fire Data and Ecoregion Boundaries for R
title: fireR • USGS Fire Data and Ecoregion Boundaries for R
text: Fast access MTBS (Monitoring Trends Burn Severity) fire perimeter data, SE FireMap burn severity mosaics, EPA/CEC ecoregion boundaries straight source.

title: fireR • USGS Fire Data and Ecoregion Boundaries for R
text: fireR provides convenient access USGS fire datasets EPA/CEC ecoregion boundaries: get_mtbs() downloads MTBS ZIP directory read_mtbs() reads, filters, returns data ZIP sf, terra::SpatVector, data.frame get_sefire() downloads SE FireMap Annual Burn Severity Mosaic ZIP(s) one years (2000–2022) get_nal1eco() / get_nal2eco() / get_nal3eco() — download load CEC North America ecoregions Levels 1–3 get_usl3eco() / get_usl4eco() — download load US EPA ecoregions Levels 3–4 (optional state boundaries) Key features: ⚡ Fast downloads — uses curl’s HTTP/2 persistent connection 🗓️ Flexible year filtering — single year (2020), range (2010:2020), specific years (c(2000, 2010, 2020)) 🗺️ Flexible output — sf, terra, plain data.frame 💾 Optional caching — skip download repeat calls

title: fireR • USGS Fire Data and Ecoregion Boundaries for R
text: development, install :
code: # Install from GitHub (once published) # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("noahweidig/fireR") devtools::install()    # run from the package root

title: fireR • USGS Fire Data and Ecoregion Boundaries for R
code: library(fireR)  fires <- read_mtbs(output = "sf") plot(fires["BurnBndAc"])

title: fireR • USGS Fire Data and Ecoregion Boundaries for R
code: tbl <- read_mtbs(geometry = FALSE)

what: Cache the download for future sessions
title: fireR • USGS Fire Data and Ecoregion Boundaries for R
code: # Cache in the default user directory fires <- read_mtbs(cache = TRUE)  # Or supply your own cache path fires <- read_mtbs(cache = "~/data/mtbs_cache")

title: fireR • USGS Fire Data and Ecoregion Boundaries for R
text: Download burn severity mosaics one years:
code: # Single year zip_path <- get_sefire(2020)  # Contiguous range zip_paths <- get_sefire(2015:2020, directory = "data/sefire")  # Specific years zip_paths <- get_sefire(c(2000, 2010, 2020))

title: fireR • USGS Fire Data and Ecoregion Boundaries for R
code: # Level 1 — broadest continental divisions na_l1 <- get_nal1eco()  # Level 2 — finer continental subdivisions na_l2 <- get_nal2eco()  # Level 3 — finest continental scale (= US EPA Level III) na_l3 <- get_nal3eco(output = "vect")

what: US EPA (Levels 3–4, with optional state boundaries)
title: fireR • USGS Fire Data and Ecoregion Boundaries for R
text: ecoregion functions accept output = "sf" (default) output = "vect" cache argument persist downloads sessions.
code: # Level 3 — without state boundaries (default) us_l3 <- get_usl3eco()  # Level 3 — with state boundaries us_l3_states <- get_usl3eco(state = TRUE)  # Level 4 — finest US subdivisions us_l4 <- get_usl4eco() us_l4_states <- get_usl4eco(state = TRUE)

title: fireR • USGS Fire Data and Ecoregion Boundaries for R
text: Download data disk first get_mtbs():
code: mtbs_dir <- get_mtbs()

what: fireR: Fast Access to MTBS, SE FireMap, and Ecoregion Data — fireR-package
title: fireR: Fast Access to MTBS, SE FireMap, and Ecoregion Data — fireR-package
text: fireR provides ergonomic entry points USGS fire data EPA/CEC ecoregion boundaries: get_mtbs() read_mtbs() MTBS (Monitoring Trends Burn Severity) composite burned-area extent data, get_sefire() Southeast FireMap Annual Burn Severity Mosaics, family ecoregion loaders (get_nal1eco(), get_nal2eco(), get_nal3eco(), get_usl3eco(), get_usl4eco()) CEC North America US EPA ecoregion boundaries. Spatial results can returned sf object, terra::SpatVector, plain data.frame.

title: fireR: Fast Access to MTBS, SE FireMap, and Ecoregion Data — fireR-package
text: get_mtbs() — download MTBS fire perimeter ZIP data. read_mtbs() — read, filter, return MTBS fire perimeters. get_sefire() — download SE FireMap Annual Burn Severity Mosaic ZIP(s) one years (2000–2022). get_nal1eco() — download load North America Level 1 Ecoregions. get_nal2eco() — download load North America Level 2 Ecoregions. get_nal3eco() — download load North America Level 3 Ecoregions. get_usl3eco() — download load US Level 3 Ecoregions. get_usl4eco() — download load US Level 4 Ecoregions.

title: fireR: Fast Access to MTBS, SE FireMap, and Ecoregion Data — fireR-package
text: Maintainer: Noah Weidig noah.weidig@ufl.edu

what: Download MTBS perimeter or occurrence data — get_mtbs
title: Download MTBS perimeter or occurrence data — get_mtbs
text: Downloads MTBS composite data ZIP archive directory. ZIP already exists overwrite = FALSE, network call made.

title: Download MTBS perimeter or occurrence data — get_mtbs
code: get_mtbs(   directory = getwd(),   dataset = c("perimeters", "occurrence"),   overwrite = FALSE,   timeout = 3600,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Download MTBS perimeter or occurrence data — get_mtbs
text: directory character(1) directory ZIP file stored. Defaults current working directory. dataset character(1) dataset download. Use "perimeters" (default) get fire perimeters polygons, "occurrence" get fire centroids points. overwrite logical(1) re-download TRUE; defaults FALSE. timeout numeric(1) download timeout seconds. MTBS ZIP ~360 MB, default 3600 (one hour). Set lower value want stricter limit. verbose logical(1) print progress messages.

title: Download MTBS perimeter or occurrence data — get_mtbs
text: character(1) path downloaded ZIP file (invisibly).

title: Download MTBS perimeter or occurrence data — get_mtbs
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ zip_path <- get_mtbs() zip_path_pts <- get_mtbs(dataset = "occurrence") } # }

what: Download and load North America Level 1 Ecoregions — get_nal1eco
title: Download and load North America Level 1 Ecoregions — get_nal1eco
text: Downloads loads Commission Environmental Cooperation (CEC) North America Level 1 Ecoregion boundaries spatial object.

title: Download and load North America Level 1 Ecoregions — get_nal1eco
code: get_nal1eco(   output = c("sf", "vect", "terra"),   cache = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Download and load North America Level 1 Ecoregions — get_nal1eco
text: output character(1) class returned spatial object. Either "sf" (default) "vect" / "terra" terra::SpatVector. cache logical(1) character(1).  Controls ZIP cached.  FALSE (default) uses temporary directory cleaned R session ends.  TRUE uses platform user cache directory (tools::R_user_dir("fireR", "cache")).  Supply directory path string specify custom location. verbose logical(1) print progress messages.

title: Download and load North America Level 1 Ecoregions — get_nal1eco
text: sf object terra::SpatVector North America Level 1 Ecoregion polygons.

title: Download and load North America Level 1 Ecoregions — get_nal1eco
text: Commission Environmental Cooperation (CEC) North American ecoregion framework divides continent hierarchical levels based similarity ecosystems type, quality, quantity environmental resources.  Level 1 represents broadest ecological divisions continent.  Data sourced US EPA / CEC ecoregion mapping programme.

title: Download and load North America Level 1 Ecoregions — get_nal1eco
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ eco <- get_nal1eco() eco_terra <- get_nal1eco(output = "vect") } # }

what: Download Southeast FireMap Annual Burn Severity Mosaics — get_sefire
title: Download Southeast FireMap Annual Burn Severity Mosaics — get_sefire
text: Downloads one Southeast FireMap (SE FireMap) Annual Burn Severity Mosaic ZIP archives USGS local directory. ZIP already exists overwrite = FALSE, network call made year.

title: Download Southeast FireMap Annual Burn Severity Mosaics — get_sefire
code: get_sefire(   years,   directory = getwd(),   overwrite = FALSE,   timeout = 3600,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Download Southeast FireMap Annual Burn Severity Mosaics — get_sefire
text: years integer vector years download.  Accepts single year (2020), contiguous range created : notation (2010:2015), vector specific years (c(2000, 2010, 2020)).  values must 2000 2022 (inclusive).  Duplicate years silently ignored. directory character(1) directory ZIP file(s) saved.  Defaults current working directory. overwrite logical(1) re-download TRUE; defaults FALSE. timeout numeric(1) download timeout seconds. Defaults 3600 (one hour). verbose logical(1) print progress messages.

title: Download Southeast FireMap Annual Burn Severity Mosaics — get_sefire
text: character vector paths downloaded ZIP files (returned invisibly).  length equals number unique years requested.

title: Download Southeast FireMap Annual Burn Severity Mosaics — get_sefire
text: southeastern United States experiences frequent wild prescribed fire activity. USGS developed gradient-boosted decision tree model trained 5,000 Composite Burn Inventory (CBI) field plots predict post-fire burn severity CBI value (0–3) across region. model ingests ARD Landsat predictors capturing first- second-order fire effects, climate norms, fire seasonality, applies trained model extent burned area identified Landsat Burned Area Product. result annual burn severity mosaic covering 78 ARD Landsat tiles across southeastern United States years 2000–2022. mosaics improve characterisation burn severity—including small prescribed fires -represented national datasets—support estimation fire-related emissions, fuel loads, aboveground carbon storage, land management activities.

title: Download Southeast FireMap Annual Burn Severity Mosaics — get_sefire
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Single year zip_path <- get_sefire(2010)  # Contiguous range (2015 through 2020) zip_paths <- get_sefire(2015:2020, directory = "data/sefire")  # Specific years only zip_paths <- get_sefire(c(2000, 2010, 2020)) } # }

what: Download and load US Level 3 Ecoregions — get_usl3eco

title: Download and load US Level 3 Ecoregions — get_usl3eco
code: get_usl3eco(   state = FALSE,   output = c("sf", "vect", "terra"),   cache = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Download and load US Level 3 Ecoregions — get_usl3eco
text: state logical(1) TRUE, dataset state boundaries dissolved ecoregion polygons returned.  Defaults FALSE. output character(1) class returned spatial object. Either "sf" (default) "vect" / "terra" terra::SpatVector. cache logical(1) character(1).  Controls ZIP cached.  FALSE (default) uses temporary directory cleaned R session ends.  TRUE uses platform user cache directory (tools::R_user_dir("fireR", "cache")).  Supply directory path string specify custom location. verbose logical(1) print progress messages.

title: Download and load US Level 3 Ecoregions — get_usl3eco
text: sf object terra::SpatVector US Level 3 Ecoregion polygons.

title: Download and load US Level 3 Ecoregions — get_usl3eco
text: US EPA ecoregion framework classifies areas United States similarity ecosystems environmental resources.  Level 3 provides finer subdivisions Level 2 regions widely used environmental assessment, monitoring, reporting regional scales. Data sourced US EPA Office Research Development.

title: Download and load US Level 3 Ecoregions — get_usl3eco
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ eco <- get_usl3eco() eco_state <- get_usl3eco(state = TRUE) eco_terra <- get_usl3eco(output = "vect") } # }

what: Read MTBS fire perimeter data — read_mtbs
title: Read MTBS fire perimeter data — read_mtbs
text: Reads MTBS fire perimeters local MTBS ZIP downloaded get_mtbs() returns either spatial object plain attribute table.

title: Read MTBS fire perimeter data — read_mtbs
code: read_mtbs(   years = NULL,   type = NULL,   geometry = TRUE,   output = c("vect", "sf", "terra"),   cache = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Read MTBS fire perimeter data — read_mtbs
text: years integer vector years keep.  Accepts single year (2020), contiguous range created : notation (2010:2020), vector specific years (c(2000, 2010, 2020)).  fires whose ignition year appears years returned.  NULL (default) returns years without filtering. type character vector incident types keep, matched Incid_Type column. Valid values "Wildfire", "Prescribed Fire", "Unknown", "Wildland Fire Use". NULL (default) returns incident types. geometry logical(1) TRUE (default) result spatial object (sf terra::SpatVector depending output). FALSE shapefile attributes returned plain data.frame geometry column dropped. output character(1) class returned spatial object. Either "vect" / "terra" (default) terra::SpatVector, "sf" sf object. Ignored geometry = FALSE. cache logical(1) character(1). Controls read_mtbs() looks downloaded ZIP file. FALSE (default) current working directory used. TRUE platform user cache directory (tools::R_user_dir("fireR", "cache")) used. Supply directory path string specify custom location. verbose logical(1) print progress messages.

title: Read MTBS fire perimeter data — read_mtbs
text: terra::SpatVector output = "vect" / "terra" geometry = TRUE. sf object output = "sf" geometry = TRUE. data.frame geometry = FALSE.

title: Read MTBS fire perimeter data — read_mtbs
text: read_mtbs() download data. Use get_mtbs() first obtain ZIP archive.

title: Read MTBS fire perimeter data — read_mtbs
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ zip_path <- get_mtbs() fires <- read_mtbs(output = "sf")  # Single year fires_2020 <- read_mtbs(years = 2020, type = "Wildfire", output = "sf")  # Contiguous range fires_recent <- read_mtbs(years = 2018:2023, output = "sf")  # Specific years only fires_sel <- read_mtbs(years = c(2010, 2015, 2020), output = "sf")  tbl <- read_mtbs(geometry = FALSE) } # }
